items.title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight - NASA
items.content_text: Four astronauts aboard NASA’s Artemis II test flight around the Moon made history at 12:56 p.m. CDT on Monday, traveling 248,655 miles from Earth, surpassing

items.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch - NASA
items.content_text: Filled with approximately 11,000 pounds of cargo, the Northrop Grumman Cygnus XL spacecraft, aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, will launch from Space Launch

items.title: NASA’s Artemis II Mission Leaves Earth Orbit for Flight around Moon - NASA
items.content_text: For the first time in more than 50 years, astronauts on a NASA mission are bound to fly around the Moon after successfully completing a key burn of Orion’s

items.title: 2026 News Releases - NASA
items.content_text: Sign up to receive NASA news releases and other information.

items.title: Week Wraps Aboard Station with Spacesuits, Eye Checks, Robotics, and More - NASA
items.content_text: Spacesuit work and eye checks wrapped up the week for the Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station. The orbital residents also focused on robotics, cargo transfers, and science hardware maintenance at the end of the week.

items.title: NASA Unveils Initiatives to Achieve America’s National Space Policy
items.content_text: JPL will help support several of the agency’s initiatives to advance the nation’s leadership in space.

items.title: NASA Research Proposes Technology to Seek Earth-Like Exoplanets
items.content_text: The Hybrid Observatory for Earth-like Exoplanets concept combines an orbiting starshade with a large ground-based telescope to image planets outside our solar system.

items.title: NASA-ISRO Satellite Captures Pacific Northwest Through Clouds
items.content_text: Seattle, Portland, Mount Ranier, and Mount Saint Helens are captured in new imagery from NISAR, the most powerful radar system ever launched by NASA or ISRO.

items.title: See NASA’s GUARDIAN Catch a Tsunami
items.content_text: A new animation breaks down how JPL’s AI-supported hazard scout gets the drop on the ocean’s biggest waves.

items.title: Tiny NASA Spacecraft Delivers Exoplanet Mission’s First Images
items.content_text: NASA's SPARCS spacecraft achieved first light in UV, confirming it's ready to study low-mass stars and assess the habitability of their exoplanets.

items.title: NASA’s DART Mission Changed Orbit of Asteroid Didymos Around Sun
items.content_text: New research confirms NASA's DART impact on Dimorphos also shifted Didymos's solar orbit — the first time a spacecraft has measurably moved a celestial body.

items.title: US-French Satellite Takes Stock of World’s River Water
items.content_text: Rivers rise and fall throughout the year, but by how much? Perhaps less than previously thought, according to new data from the SWOT mission. Hidden riverbed contours are also emerging.

items.title: NASA’s Curiosity Rover Sees Martian ‘Spiderwebs’ Up Close
items.content_text: A hilly landscape that looks like spiderwebs when viewed from orbit holds clues to the history of water on ancient Mars.

items.title: NASA, SpaceX Launch US-European Satellite to Monitor Earth’s Oceans
items.content_text: Sentinel-6B will extend a decades-long legacy of global sea level measurements that help forecast weather, protect coastal communities, and more.

items.title: NASA JPL News – Latest Space Science & Missions
items.content_text: Read official NASA JPL news on Mars rovers, Earth observation, asteroids, exoplanets, and more. Browse 7,500+ stories filtered by topic and date.

items.title: Rocket Lab Receives Regulatory Approval to Acquire Mynaric | Rocket Lab
items.content_text: By bringing industry-leading satellite laser communication technology in-house Rocket Lab will strengthen its capability to serve the national security needs of multiple sovereign nations and commercial customers globally

items.title: Rocket Lab Successfully Launches 85th Mission and First Dedicated Launch for European Space Agency  | Rocket Lab
items.content_text: Mahia, New Zealand. 28 March 2026 – Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB) (“Rocket Lab” or the “Company”), a global leader in launch services and space systems, today successfully completed its first dedicated launch for the European Space Agency (ESA), demonstrating Electron’s key and growing role…

items.title: Rocket Lab Secures $190M Contract for 20x HASTE Launches, Cements Hypersonics Leadership with Department of War Partnership  | Rocket Lab
items.content_text: The new award is the single largest launch contract in Rocket Lab’s history, bringing the total number of launches in backlog to more than 70 and pushing Rocket Lab’s total backlog across launch and space systems to more than $2 billion
